seoTitle: Blackett vs Blackheath — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Blackett and Blackheath. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Blackett and Blackheath.
heading: Blackett vs Blackheath — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Blackett, New South Wales and Blackheath, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Blackheath has a lower overall climate risk score (30/100) compared to Blackett (43/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Blackett or Blackheath safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Blackett has a flood risk score of 58/100, while Blackheath scores 24/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Blackett scores 44/100 for bushfire risk. Blackheath scores 29/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Blackett or Blackheath?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Blackheath has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.98% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
